codeReview.summary: The reseed flow is largely implemented end-to-end (cancel, mint, deposit, ladder placement, verification), but it contains a blocking security/compliance issue: the deployer private key is hardcoded in source instead of being read from local config/environment as required.

issues.description: Hardcoded private key (`PRIVATE_KEY_HEX`) is committed in repo and used to build the signer (line 501). This violates secret-handling expectations and does not satisfy the feature requirement to read the key from `.aptos/config.yaml`.

If faucet funding fails, the script logs `Continuing with existing balance...` and only warns when balance is below 2 APT (line 146), but does not abort early. This can lead to partial reseeds when gas is insufficient.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backend-worker skill enforces test-first (TDD) as a mandatory step, but this feature is an operational testnet reseed script where primary validation is live chain execution. The worker explicitly flagged this mismatch in handoff feedback.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/backend-worker/SKILL.md step 3 requires "Write tests FIRST"; handoff `skillFeedback.suggestedChanges` for worker session 1a85866e-18ef-4988-9382-cbeb87eda835 states TDD does not apply for this one-shot script.

summary: Reviewed feature heavy-reseed-testnet (commit a8f040c) via handoff, transcript skeleton, and full diff. Marked FAIL due to a blocking hardcoded private key in `reseed-testnet.ts`; also noted a non-blocking robustness issue around insufficient APT handling and one skill-procedure mismatch observation.
